title: Chimichurri Chicken Thighs with Garlicky White Beans & Quinoa
description: Grilled bone-in chicken thighs over herby chimichurri, with smoky sautéed white beans and fluffy quinoa. Three components, one great plate.

ingredients.name: cannellini or white beans, drained and rinsed
water or low-sodium chicken broth (for quinoa)

instructions.text: Make the chimichurri: finely chop parsley by hand or pulse 4–5 times in a food processor -- do not puree. Combine with 4 minced garlic cloves, red wine vinegar, olive oil, oregano, red pepper flakes, and a generous pinch of salt and pepper. Stir and set aside -- it needs at least 15 minutes to meld.
Season chicken thighs: pat completely dry and season generously all over with salt and pepper.

Grill chicken skin-side down over medium-high heat. Don't move them -- let the skin render and release naturally, about 10–12 minutes. Flip and cook another 10–12 minutes until internal temp reaches 165°F. Rest 5 minutes before serving.

Cook quinoa: combine quinoa and water or broth in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce to lowest simmer, cover, and cook 15 minutes until water is absorbed. Remove from heat and rest covered 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork.

Build the white beans: heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add 4 minced garlic cloves and rosemary. Cook 60–90 seconds until fragrant but not brown. Add beans and smoked paprika, stir to coat. Pour in chicken broth and let it reduce 3–4 minutes, stirring occasionally. Squeeze in lemon juice, season, and remove rosemary sprig.

Spoon quinoa as the base. Add a generous scoop of white beans alongside. Place chicken thigh on top. Spoon chimichurri liberally over the chicken -- and over the beans and quinoa too. Extra chimichurri on the table.

notes:
Chimichurri improves the longer it sits -- make it first, or even the day before.

Don't rush the skin on the grill. Letting it release naturally gives you crispy skin without tearing. If it's sticking, it needs more time.

The white beans can be made ahead and reheated -- they hold well and actually improve as the flavors meld.
